basics.name: Manon Sarthou
basics.label: Plant Biologist
basics.summary: Biologist specialized in the reaction of plants to metal stress

location.address: Bât. B22 Biologie végétale translationnelle, chemin de la Vallée 4

work.name: Translational Plant Biology, InBios, University of Liège, Belgium
work.summary: I am working on a FNRS project at Marc Hanikenne's lab (ULiege, Belgium) in collaboration with Nathalie Verbruggen (ULB, Belgium). My work focuses on understanding plant responses to zinc deficiency, zinc excess, and cadmium exposure, through physiological, transcriptomic, and computational analyses.

education.name: Étude des mécanismes moléculaires liés à l’accumulation d’uranium dans Arabidopsis thaliana.

education.area: Plant biology and interactions with microorganisms ('ADAM' Master's Degree)
education.name: Characterization of a new protein methyltransferase involved in the response of plants to metal stress.

publications.name: [UNDER REVIEW] Specific redox and iron homeostasis responses in the root tip of Arabidopsis upon zinc excess.
[UNDER REVIEW] A multi-omics analysis of Arabidopsis thaliana root tips under Cd exposure: A role of HY5 in limiting accumulation.
Calcium-permeable cation channels are involved in uranium uptake in Arabidopsis thaliana.
Development of a metalloproteomic approach to analyse the response of Arabidopsis cells to uranium stress.
Protein lysine methylation contributes to modulating the response of sensitive and tolerant Arabidopsis species to cadmium stress.

communications.name: Increased camalexin synthesis in Arabidopsis contributes to the Zn excess response.

teaching.area: Plant physiology to undergraduates, practicals (12h) and tutorials (12h)
